mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat to falling, equity discount rates ease and valuation multiples expand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because real-rate relief then dominates inflation fear.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher than baseline when FEDFUNDS declines or stays unchanged for 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the signal loses positive rank correlation in a rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening labor demand and slower earnings momentum, which reduces risk appetite and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect is directional only when the unemployment move is not offset by a concurrent employment surge in PAYEMS.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if more than 60% of triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns across any 24-month walk-forward segment.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized pickup in RSAFS and GDPC1 reflects stronger final demand and revenue growth, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 performance over the next quarter. The effect should be strongest when both series are above their prior-year trend.
failure_condition: Reject if combined demand-strength episodes do not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if the interaction term between RSAFS and GDPC1 is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and profit momentum, which leads S&P 500 returns lower over the next month as cyclical equities reprice. The signal fails when the decline is isolated and housing activity, captured by HOUST, is accelerating strongly.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO does not predict lower 20-day returns, or if positive forward returns dominate when INDPRO is weak despite no compensating housing weakness.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in HOUST indicates stronger interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, which improves cyclical growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is most reliable when housing starts are rising from a depressed base.
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing HOUST episodes fail to produce positive average 5-day returns, or if the hit rate stays below 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS still below inflation compresses real policy rates and later forces a risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is active only when inflation is accelerating faster than the policy rate can adjust.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3pp annualized above its 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes do not coincide with lower 20-day equity returns, or if the interaction between CPIAUCSL acceleration and low FEDFUNDS is not negative in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling PAYEMS combined with low UNRATE reflects a mature labor market that supports earnings without wage stress, which should allow equities to grind higher over the next quarter. The signal fails when unemployment falls because of labor-force exit rather than genuine hiring strength.
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment episodes with stable PAYEMS do not deliver above-baseline 60-day returns, or if positive returns disappear when PAYEMS trends down despite low UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand deceleration that should pressure earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect is strongest when both series deteriorate together rather than separately.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decline versus their prior 2-quarter or 2-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if joint demand slowdowns do not produce negative 20-day returns, or if the joint effect is no stronger than the individual series effects in rolling walk-forward tests.
